_comment: Catetus MCP — Cline (VS Code extension) config.
Cline stores MCP server config in cline_mcp_settings.json. Open it via:
1. Click the Cline icon in the VS Code sidebar
2. Click the 'MCP Servers' button at the top of the Cline panel
3. Click 'Configure MCP Servers'  →  this opens cline_mcp_settings.json
(Or find it manually under your VS Code user/extensions storage.)
Paste the `mcpServers` block below into that file. Cline will hot-reload
the server (you should see it appear in the MCP Servers list within seconds).
Two variants below: (1) local stdio (free + paid via env) and
(2) hosted HTTP at mcp.catetus.com (paid only). Pick ONE.
FREE TIER (stdio, no key): analyze, list_presets, list_scenes, get_scene,
PAID TIER (key set): also encode, score_fidelity, repack, predict_quality,

catetus._comment: FREE + PAID via stdio. Omit the env block to use the free tier only.

catetus-hosted-disabled._comment: Alternative: hosted HTTP at mcp.catetus.com. Cline supports remote MCP via the `url` field. Rename this key to `catetus` and delete the stdio entry above to use this one.
